---
title: "Research Capability"
---

This section gives a quick look at how I work as a researcher, from building between-subjects experiments and working in RStudio to turning ideas into clear research outputs. It shows how I design studies, work with data, and bring research into publishable form.

# Field stimuli

Here is one example from a field experiment using a between-subjects design, where I tested whether including human presence in short video stimuli increased consumersâ€™ embodied mental simulation and, in turn, encouraged click-through behavior.

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Human Presence
<iframe width="100%" height="315"
src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/YC0EvhrqDv4"
title="Human Presence"
frameborder="0"
allowfullscreen>
</iframe>
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Human Absence
<iframe width="100%" height="315"
src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/1uKujcBwDbk"
title="Human Absence"
frameborder="0"
allowfullscreen>
</iframe>
:::

::::

## Experimental Research
This section presents one experimental study in RStudio to show how I design experiments, run analyses, and interpret results through reproducible code.
<style> pre code {font-size: 12px;}
</style>
```{r, echo=FALSE}
# Packages
options(scipen = 20)
library(rio)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)

cleanup <- theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
                 panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
                 panel.background = element_blank(),
                 axis.line.x = element_line(color = "black"),
                 axis.line.y = element_line(color = "black"),
                 legend.key = element_rect(fill ="white"),
                 text = element_text(family = "serif", size = 14),
                 plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))

# Import data

data_demo <- import("D:/Website_tuantoddcao/tuantoddcao.github.io/Study1B.xlsx")

data_demo$edu <- factor(data_demo$edu,
                     levels = c(1:9),
                     labels = c ("< Primary", "Primary", "Some secondary",
                                 "Secondary","Vocational", "Some university", 
                                 "Bachelor's", "Graduate/Professional", 
                                 "Not disclosed"))

my_colors <- c("#0000ff", "#9d02d7", "#cd34b5", "#ea5f94", "#fa8775", "#ffb14e", "#ffd700")

plot_demo <- function(data_use, varname, title_text) {
  
  tab <- data_use %>%
    count(.data[[varname]]) %>%
    rename(category = 1) %>%
    arrange(desc(n)) %>%
    mutate(rank = row_number())
  
  p <- ggplot(
    tab,
    aes(
      x = n,
      y = reorder(category, n),
      fill = factor(rank),
      text = paste0(category, "<br>Participants: ", n)
    )
  ) +
    geom_col(width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
    geom_text(aes(label = n), hjust = -0.1, size = 4) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = rep(my_colors, length.out = nrow(tab))) +
    labs(
      title = title_text,
      x = "The number of participants",
      y = NULL
    ) +
    cleanup +
    theme(axis.title.y = element_blank()) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, max(tab$n) * 1.15))
  
  ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text")
}

plot_demo(data_demo, "edu", "Education")

```
## Structural Equation Modeling and Machine-Learning Analyses
This section presents SEM and machine learning analyses in RStudio to show how I test models, assess predictive performance, and validate findings through code.

### Structural Equation Modeling

<style> pre code {font-size: 12px;}
</style>
```{r, eval=FALSE}
# Preparation
options(scipen = 20)
library(psych)
library(rio)
library(ggplot2)
library(moments)
library(seminr)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(car)
library(table1)
library(flextable)
library(officer)
library(glmnet)
library(patchwork)
library(stringr)
library(GGally)
library(bnlearn)
library(tidyr)
library(glmnet)
library(caret)
library(randomForest)
cleanup <- theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
                 panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
                 panel.background = element_blank(),
                 axis.line.x = element_line(color = "black"),
                 axis.line.y = element_line(color = "black"),
                 legend.key = element_rect(fill ="white"),
                 text = element_text(family = "serif", size = 14),
                 plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))

freq_percent <- function(x) {
  freq <- table(x)
  percentage <- prop.table(freq)*100
  percentage <- round(percentage, 2)
  result <- data.frame(
    value = names(freq),
    Frequency = as.vector(freq),
    Percentage = as.vector(percentage)
  )
  return(result)
}

# Data imported
data <- import("D:/Website_tuantoddcao/tuantoddcao.github.io/Study1B.xlsx")
summary(data)

# Descriptive statistics

data$agegroup <- cut(data$age,
                       breaks = c(17, 27, 43, 59, Inf),
                       labels = c("18 to 27","28 to 43", "44 to 59", "60 or over"))
data$gender <- factor(data$gender,
                        levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
                        labels = c("Female", "Male", "Non binary / Third gender", "Prefer not to say"))
data$edu <- factor(data$edu,
                     levels = c(1:9),
                     labels = c ("Less than primary", "Primary", "Some secondary",
                                 "Secondary","Vocational or similar", "Some University but no degree", 
                                 "University - Bachelors Degree", "Graduate or professional degree", 
                                 "Prefer not to say"))
data$income <- factor(data$income,
                        levels = c(1:6),
                        labels = c("Less than $30,000", "$30,000 - $49,999", "$50,000 - $79,999",
                                   "$80,000 - $149,999", "$150,000 - $199,999", "More than $200,000"))
data$employment <-factor(data$employment,
                           levels = c(1:6),
                           labels = c("Working full-time", "Working part-time", "Unemployed and looking for work",
                                      "A homemaker or stay-at-home parent",
                                      "Student", "Retired"))
freq_percent(data$gender)
describe(data$age)

# Demographic graph

my_colors <- c("#0000ff", "#9d02d7", "#cd34b5", "#ea5f94", "#fa8775", "#ffb14e", "#ffd700")

plot_demo <- function(data_use, varname, title_text) {

  tab <- data_use %>%
    dplyr::count(.data[[varname]]) %>%
    rename(category = 1) %>%
    arrange(desc(n)) %>%
    mutate(rank = row_number())
  
 ggplot(tab, aes(x = n, y = reorder(category, n), fill = factor(rank))) +
    geom_col(width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
    geom_text(aes(label = n), hjust = -0.1, size = 4) +
   scale_fill_manual(
  values = rep(my_colors, length.out = nrow(tab))
) +
    labs(
      title = title_text,
      x = "The number of participants",
      y = NULL
    ) +
    cleanup +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title.y = element_blank()
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, max(tab$n) * 1.15))
}

p_age <- plot_demo(data, "agegroup", "Age group")
p_gender <- plot_demo(data, "gender", "Gender")
p_edu <- plot_demo(data, "edu", "Education")

p_income <- plot_demo(data, "income", "Income")
p_emp <- plot_demo(data, "employment", "Employment status")

ggsave("gender.png", p_gender, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 600)
ggsave("age.png", p_age, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 600)
ggsave("education.png", p_edu, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 600)
ggsave("income.png", p_income, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 600)
ggsave("employment.png", p_emp, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 600)

# Scatter plot
data_scatter <- data

data_scatter <- data.frame(
  E.use = rowMeans(data[, c("easyuse_1","easyuse_2","easyuse_3")], na.rm = TRUE),
  Personalization = rowMeans(data[, c("personal_1","personal_2","personal_3","personal_4")], na.rm = TRUE),
  R.advantage = rowMeans(data[, c("advantage_1","advantage_2","advantage_3")], na.rm = TRUE),
  Observability = rowMeans(data[, c("visibility_1","visibility_2","visibility_3")], na.rm = TRUE),
  Compatibility = rowMeans(data[, c("compatibility_1","compatibility_2","compatibility_3","compatibility_4")], na.rm = TRUE),
  Reliance = rowMeans(data[, c("reliance_1","reliance_2","reliance_3")], na.rm = TRUE),
  Purchase.intent = rowMeans(data[, c("purchase_1","purchase_2","purchase_3")], na.rm = TRUE),
  Ad.skepticism = rowMeans(data[, c("skepticism_1","skepticism_2","skepticism_3","skepticism_4","skepticism_5")], na.rm = TRUE)
)

pair_df <- data_scatter[, c(
  "E.use",
  "Personalization",
  "R.advantage",
  "Observability",
  "Compatibility",
  "Reliance",
  "Purchase.intent",
  "Ad.skepticism"
)]

scatter_plot <- ggpairs(
  pair_df,
  columns = 1:8,
  upper = list(continuous = wrap("density", alpha = 0.6, color = "#3B4CC0")),
  lower = list(continuous = wrap("points", alpha = 0.4, size = 0.8, color = "#DC143C")),
  diag  = list(continuous = wrap("densityDiag", color = "#008000"))
) +

# Correlation
cor(pair_df, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# Outliers
## Mahalanobis
data_construct <- data.frame(
  E.use = rowMeans(data[, c("easyuse_1","easyuse_2","easyuse_3")], na.rm = TRUE),
  Personalization = rowMeans(data[, c("personal_1","personal_2","personal_3","personal_4")], na.rm = TRUE),
  R.advantage = rowMeans(data[, c("advantage_1","advantage_2","advantage_3")], na.rm = TRUE),
  S.visibility = rowMeans(data[, c("visibility_1","visibility_2","visibility_3")], na.rm = TRUE),
  P.compatibility = rowMeans(data[, c("compatibility_1","compatibility_2","compatibility_3","compatibility_4")], na.rm = TRUE),
  P.reliance = rowMeans(data[, c("reliance_1","reliance_2","reliance_3")], na.rm = TRUE),
  Purchase.intent = rowMeans(data[, c("purchase_1","purchase_2","purchase_3")], na.rm = TRUE),
  Ad.skepticism = rowMeans(data[, c("skepticism_1","skepticism_2","skepticism_3","skepticism_4","skepticism_5")], na.rm = TRUE)
)

data_md <- na.omit(data_construct)

mahal <- mahalanobis(
  data_md,
  colMeans(data_md),
  cov(data_md)
)

cutmahal <- qchisq(1 - 0.001, df = ncol(data_md))
cutmahal
badmahal <- as.numeric(mahal > cutmahal)
table(badmahal)

## Leverage

outlier_model <- lm(
  rep(1, nrow(data_md)) ~ .,
  data = data_md
)

k <- length(outlier_model$coefficients) - 1
leverage <- hatvalues(outlier_model)
cutleverage <- (2 * k + 2) / nrow(data_md)
cutleverage
badleverage <- as.numeric(leverage > cutleverage)
table(badleverage)

## Cooks distance
cooks <- cooks.distance(outlier_model)
cutcooks <- 4/(nrow(data_md) - k - 1)
cutcooks
badcooks <- as.numeric(cooks > cutcooks)
table(badcooks)

## Noout_data

totalout <- badmahal + badcooks + badleverage
data_noout <- subset(data, totalout <2)
dim(data)
dim(data_noout)

# Table

outlier_table <- data.frame(
  No = data$ID,
  Mahalanobis = badmahal,
  Leverage    = badleverage,
  CooksD      = badcooks
)

outlier_table$TotalFlags <- 
  outlier_table$Mahalanobis +
  outlier_table$Leverage +
  outlier_table$CooksD

outlier_table$Removed <- ifelse(outlier_table$TotalFlags >= 2, "Yes", "No")

table_out <- subset(outlier_table, TotalFlags > 0)
ft <- flextable(table_out)
ft <- autofit(ft)
doc <- read_docx()
doc <- body_add_flextable(doc, value = ft)
print(doc, target = "Outlier_JIC.docx")

# Outer loadings
Outer loading â‰Ą .70 â†’ ideal, retain
.40â€“.69 â†’ retain only if CR and AVE are acceptable
< .40 â†’ drop

mm <- constructs(
  composite("easeuse",        multi_items("easyuse_", 1:3)),
  composite("personalization",multi_items("personal_", 1:4)),
  composite("reladv",         multi_items("advantage_", 1:3)),
  composite("visibility",     multi_items("visibility_", 1:3)),
  composite("compatibility",  multi_items("compatibility_", 1:4)),

    composite("reliance",       multi_items("reliance_", 1:3)),
  composite("purchase",       multi_items("purchase_", 1:3)),

  composite("ad_skepticism", multi_items("skepticism_", c(1,2,4))),
  interaction_term(iv = "reliance", moderator = "ad_skepticism", method = two_stage)
)

sm <- relationships(
  paths(from = c("easeuse",
                 "personalization",
                 "reladv",
                 "visibility",
                 "compatibility"),
        to = "reliance"),
  paths(from = c("reliance", "ad_skepticism", "reliance*ad_skepticism"), to = "purchase")
)

pls_model <- estimate_pls(
  data = data_noout,
  measurement_model = mm,
  structural_model  = sm
)

outer_loadings <- pls_model$outer_loadings
outer_loadings

ol_long <- as.data.frame(as.table(pls_model$outer_loadings))
ol_long %>%
  filter(Freq > 0) %>%     
  group_by(Var2) %>%      
  summarise(
    min_loading = min(Freq),
    max_loading = max(Freq)
  )

# Composite Reliability (rhoC), Cronbachâ€™s alpha (Î±), rhoA, and AVE
summary(pls_model)$reliability

# Discriminant validity
pls_model_full <- estimate_pls(
  data = data_noout,
  measurement_model = mm,
  structural_model  = sm,
  inner_weights = path_weighting
)

model_summary <- summary(pls_model_full)
htmt_results <- model_summary$validity$htmt
print(htmt_results)

# VIF

summary(pls_model_full)$vif

# RÂ˛ 
pls_model_full$rSquared

# Bootstrapping
check_hypothesis <- bootstrap_model(
  seminr_model = pls_model_full,
  nboot = 5000,
  cores = parallel::detectCores() - 1
)

summary(check_hypothesis)
```
### Mahine Learning analysis

<style> pre code {font-size: 12px;}
</style>
```{r, eval=FALSE}
make_ml_data <- function(data_noout) {
  df <- data.frame(
    E.use = rowMeans(data_noout[, c("easyuse_1","easyuse_2","easyuse_3")], na.rm = TRUE),
    Personalization = rowMeans(data_noout[, c("personal_1","personal_2","personal_3","personal_4")], na.rm = TRUE),
    R.advantage = rowMeans(data_noout[, c("advantage_1","advantage_2","advantage_3")], na.rm = TRUE),
    S.visibility = rowMeans(data_noout[, c("visibility_1","visibility_2","visibility_3")], na.rm = TRUE),
    P.compatibility = rowMeans(data_noout[, c("compatibility_1","compatibility_2","compatibility_3","compatibility_4")], na.rm = TRUE),
    Reliance = rowMeans(data_noout[, c("reliance_1","reliance_2","reliance_3")], na.rm = TRUE),
    Purchase.intent = rowMeans(data_noout[, c("purchase_1","purchase_2","purchase_3")], na.rm = TRUE),
    Ad.skepticism = rowMeans(data_noout[, c("skepticism_1","skepticism_2","skepticism_3","skepticism_4","skepticism_5")], na.rm = TRUE)
  )

  df <- na.omit(df)

  # mean-center for interaction (no scaling)
  df[, c("Reliance","Ad.skepticism")] <- scale(df[, c("Reliance","Ad.skepticism")],
                                              center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)
  df$Reliance_x_Ad.skepticism <- df$Reliance * df$Ad.skepticism
  df
}

data_ml <- make_ml_data(data_noout)

X <- model.matrix(
  Purchase.intent ~ E.use + Personalization + R.advantage + S.visibility + P.compatibility +
    Reliance + Ad.skepticism + Reliance_x_Ad.skepticism,
  data = data_ml
)[, -1]

y <- data_ml$Purchase.intent

## 1) Elastic Net (10-fold CV) + final refit at lambda.1se

fit_enet <- function(X, y, alpha = 0.5, nfolds = 10, seed = 123) {
  set.seed(seed)

  cv_fit <- cv.glmnet(
    x = X, y = y,
    alpha = alpha,
    nfolds = nfolds,
    standardize = TRUE,
    keep = TRUE
  )
  idx <- which(cv_fit$lambda == cv_fit$lambda.1se)
  pred_oof <- as.numeric(cv_fit$fit.preval[, idx])

  rmse <- sqrt(mean((y - pred_oof)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
  mae  <- mean(abs(y - pred_oof), na.rm = TRUE)
  r2   <- cor(y, pred_oof, use = "complete.obs")^2

  final_fit <- glmnet(
    x = X, y = y,
    alpha = alpha,
    lambda = cv_fit$lambda.1se,
    standardize = TRUE
  )

  list(
    cv = cv_fit,
    lambda_1se = cv_fit$lambda.1se,
    oof = list(rmse = rmse, mae = mae, r2 = r2, pred = pred_oof),
    final = final_fit,
    coef = coef(final_fit)
  )
}

enet_res <- fit_enet(X, y, alpha = 0.5, nfolds = 10, seed = 123)

enet_res$lambda_1se
enet_res$oof[c("rmse","mae","r2")]
enet_res$coef

## Random Forest (caret 10-fold CV) + variable importance

fit_rf_cv <- function(X, y, k = 10, tuneLength = 5, ntree = 1000, seed = 123) {
  set.seed(seed)

  ctrl <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = k)

  rf_cv <- train(
    x = X, y = y,
    method = "rf",
    trControl = ctrl,
    tuneLength = tuneLength,
    ntree = ntree,
    importance = TRUE
  )

  list(
    cv = rf_cv,
    results = rf_cv$results[, c("mtry","RMSE","Rsquared","MAE")],
    best = rf_cv$bestTune,
    final_model = rf_cv$finalModel,
    varimp = varImp(rf_cv, scale = TRUE)
  )
}

rf_res <- fit_rf_cv(X, y, k = 10, tuneLength = 5, ntree = 1000, seed = 123)

rf_res$results
rf_res$best
rf_res$varimp

## Robustness check: permutation importance (CV-consistent)
rf_perm_importance_cv <- function(X, y, seed = 123, k = 10, ntree = 1000, tuneLength = 5) {
  set.seed(seed)

  folds <- createFolds(y, k = k, returnTrain = TRUE)
  ctrl <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = k, index = folds)

  # tune once to get best mtry under the same CV setup
  rf_cv <- train(
    x = X, y = y,
    method = "rf",
    trControl = ctrl,
    tuneLength = tuneLength,
    ntree = ntree,
    importance = TRUE
  )
  best_mtry <- rf_cv$bestTune$mtry

  inc_rmse <- sapply(colnames(X), function(v) {
    fold_increases <- numeric(length(folds))

    for (i in seq_along(folds)) {
      tr_idx <- folds[[i]]
      te_idx <- setdiff(seq_len(nrow(X)), tr_idx)

      Xtr <- X[tr_idx, , drop = FALSE]
      ytr <- y[tr_idx]
      Xte <- X[te_idx, , drop = FALSE]
      yte <- y[te_idx]

      set.seed(seed + i)
      rf_fit <- randomForest(x = Xtr, y = ytr, ntree = ntree, mtry = best_mtry)

      pred_base <- predict(rf_fit, Xte)
      rmse_base <- sqrt(mean((yte - pred_base)^2))

      Xte_perm <- Xte
      Xte_perm[, v] <- sample(Xte_perm[, v])
      pred_perm <- predict(rf_fit, Xte_perm)
      rmse_perm <- sqrt(mean((yte - pred_perm)^2))

      fold_increases[i] <- rmse_perm - rmse_base
    }

    mean(fold_increases)
  })

  permu <- data.frame(Predictor = names(inc_rmse), IncRMSE = as.numeric(inc_rmse))
  permu <- permu[order(-permu$IncRMSE), ]

  list(best_mtry = best_mtry, permutation_importance = permu, rf_cv = rf_cv)
}

perm_res <- rf_perm_importance_cv(X, y, seed = 123, k = 10, ntree = 1000, tuneLength = 5)

perm_res$best_mtry
head(perm_res$permutation_importance, 20)
```
